layer_map.what: Add `max-width:220px` to the `.tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper` cell rule (the design `.tab` cell); base `.tabs__tab-label` ellipsis rule reused unchanged
Remove the divergent `.tabbar .tabs__tab-label { max-width:200px }` cap so the single cap lives on the cell

Add computed-style assertion (wrapper `max-width` resolves to 220px) to the existing wrapper-geometry block; add a long-title fixture + a no-growth test (cell ≤220px, label shows ellipsis); reuse TabbarFidelityFixture

key_design_decisions.decision: Which element carries the width cap
key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Cap the CELL (`.tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper`) at 220px; remove the label cap
key_design_decisions.why: Contract §5 places `max-width` on the `.tab` cell; capping the cell makes width independent of method-chip width and fires the existing label ellipsis (AC-1/AC-6/AC-7). Not a departure — aligns code to the contract
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Bump label cap to 220px in place: leaves cell width = chip+label+padding (varies with chip, can exceed 220px), fails AC-6/AC-9

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: In Tabs.css, inside the existing `.tabbar`-scoped override block (NOT TabBar.css)
key_design_decisions.why: The `.tabbar` cell-geometry overrides (gap/padding/border at Tabs.css:421) already live here; the cap belongs with them. `.tabbar`-scoping keeps bare `<Tabs>` consumers uncapped (AC-3). Consistent with the feature-005 `.tabbar`-scoping precedent — not a departure
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Put the rule in TabBar.css: splits cell-geometry across two files, breaks the Tabs.css-owns-`.tabbar`-cell-geometry grouping

key_design_decisions.decision: Truncation machinery for the relocated cap
key_design_decisions.why: §3.6 search-before-building: the canonical rule already matches contract B1 and fires once the cell is capped. Removing the label cap drops only its `max-width`, not the ellipsis triple (which lives on the base rule)
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Add fresh truncation declarations: duplicates an existing rule, violates DRY/§3.6

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Literal `220px` (resolved contract value) with a comment citing contract §5
key_design_decisions.why: Not a departure — the prior cap was also a literal `200px`; tokens.css has no per-component width token; the contract asserts geometric caps as resolves-to-the-literal. The comment satisfies AC-10
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: New width token in tokens.css: out of scope, single call-site, over-engineers a one-off cap

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Playwright CT computed-style equality (220px) + long-title fixture no-growth test, reusing TabbarFidelityFixture
key_design_decisions.why: §3.4: jsdom cannot resolve computed layout, so the cap is proven in a real browser. The fixture already loads tokens.css/TabBar.css/Shell.css/soft-mstyle, reproducing the cascade (closes the research Open Uncertainty). Long-title fixture avoids the short-title false-pass (§9 Risk). Satisfies AC-7/AC-8/AC-9
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Vitest/jsdom unit test: cannot resolve computed `max-width`/layout — false signal

file_impact.what_changes: Add `max-width: 220px;` to the `.tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper` rule (≈:421), with a comment citing design-fidelity-contract §5 and noting the cap moved off the label onto the cell (AC-1, AC-6, AC-10)

Remove the `max-width: 200px;` declaration from `.tabbar .tabs__tab-label` (≈:88); the base Tabs.css:196 rule retains the overflow/ellipsis/nowrap, so the rule's remaining declarations (if any) stay or the now-redundant block is dropped (AC-2)
Add a long-title fixture variant (or extend TabbarFidelityFixture) so a tab's title overflows the 220px cell, enabling the no-growth/ellipsis assertion

Add a computed-style assertion that `.tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper` `max-width` resolves to 220px in the existing AC-15 wrapper-geometry block; add a no-growth test (long-title tab stays ≤220px and its label computed `text-overflow` is ellipsis) (AC-7, AC-8, AC-9)

risks.risk: Unscoped cap leaks to bare `<Tabs>` consumers (section-tabs, PrimitivesDemo) and regresses them
risks.mitigation: Add the cap only inside the `.tabbar`-scoped selector (005 precedent); AC-3 asserts bare consumers stay uncapped; no change to the base `.tabs__tab-wrapper`

risks.risk: Removing the label cap also drops its overflow/ellipsis/nowrap, breaking truncation if the base rule lacks them
risks.mitigation: Confirm the base Tabs.css:196 ellipsis triple is present before removal; keep the AC-16 label flex/overflow CT assertions green; assert ellipsis on the long-title fixture

risks.risk: The 220px cap clips the active accent pseudo-element or the overflow chevron
risks.mitigation: Rely on the existing `.tabs.tabbar { overflow:visible }` rule; keep the 005 active-pseudo + screenshot CT assertions green; design-auditor runtime pass at /review confirms no clipping

risks.risk: A short-title CT fixture never triggers truncation → false pass on the no-growth assertion
risks.mitigation: Add an explicit long-title fixture; assert both the 220px cap and the rendered ellipsis; baseline the width CT with realistic content, not empty-vs-filled

risks.risk: A future method-chip width change makes the title truncate sooner inside the fixed cell
risks.mitigation: Accepted — the cell cap is fixed and the label flexes; chip width is bounded; earlier truncation is the intended overflow behaviour

dependencies: None. No package to install, no service to configure, no environment variable. Reuses the existing Tabs.css, TabBar.css, tokens.css, and the established Playwright CT fidelity harness (TabbarFidelityFixture).
